member.name: Ben Obese-Jecty
member.synopsis: Ben Obese-Jecty is the Conservative MP for Huntingdon , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: National Infrastructure and Service Transformation Authority: Annual Reports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, pursuant to the answer of 1 July 2026 to question 13286 on Defence: Procurement, on what date the National Infrastructure and Service Transformation Authority Annual Report will be published.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The NISTA Major Projects Annual Report (2025-26) will be published later this month. When published, the report will be available both via an HTML page on GOV.uk and a public interactive dashboard.

writtenQuestions.heading: Investment: Strait of Hormuz
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, with reference to the Defence Investment Plan, published on 30 June 2026, paragraph 43, of the £120m investment in the Multinational Hormuz Mission has come from the contingency reserve.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK and France stand ready to deploy the wider Multinational Military Mission to support freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. The Chancellor has approved access to the HMT reserve to deploy additional capabilities in the Middle East for this ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Clyde Naval Base
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, with reference to the Defence Investment Plan, published on 30 June 2026, page 30, what progress has he made in acquiring three floating docks and infrastructure at Faslane.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan announced Programme Euston, a multi-billion-pound investment that aims to deliver resilient out of water engineering capability at HMNB Clyde. This will support all in service Royal Navy nuclear-powered submarines and assure th...

writtenQuestions.heading: F-35 Aircraft: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, pursuant to the answer of 30 June 2026 to question 12605 on F-35 Aircraft: Procurement, what are the additional cost estimates.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The cost differences associated with the F-35B to A variant swap in logistics, infrastructure and training are understood and have been considered in cost estimates. Any additional costs cannot be finalised until the delivery schedules are agreed.

writtenQuestions.heading: Typhoon Aircraft
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, with reference to the Defence Investment Plan, published on 30 June 2026, paragraph 73, what is the scope of the Typhoon Long Term Evolution programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Typhoon Long Term Evolution (LTE) programme is the ongoing programme to sustain and progressively enhance the Typhoon force so that it remains operationally relevant into the 2040s and can continue to meet the UK's air defence and combat air requireme...

writtenQuestions.heading: Ballistic Missile Defence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, with reference to the Defence Investment Plan, published on 30 June 2026, page 28, what his planned timetable is for delivering the Integrated Air, Space and Missile Defence Operations Centre.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Strategic Defence Review committed to strengthening the UK's homeland Integrated Air and Missile Defence, now realised through the Defence Investment Plan with £790 million of additional investment. This funding invests in an Integrated Air and Space ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Military Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, whether Projects ASGARD, BOYD, NEXUS and the Digital Targeting Web are interoperable.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Digital Targeting Web (DTW) has interoperability as one of its core design principles and will use this to guide and cohere existing projects and programmes. Projects ASGARD, BOYD and NEXUS which will form part of the DTW also have interoperability in...

writtenQuestions.heading: Warships: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what is the Planning Assumption for Service Entry of Type 91.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan announced the development of the Type 91 uncrewed missile platform as part of the Royal Navy’s future Hybrid Navy capability. Detailed requirements and delivery timelines remain subject to ongoing concept and assessment work. C...

writtenQuestions.heading: Challenger Tanks: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, pursuant to the answer of 30 June 2026 to question 12407 on Challenger Tanks: Procurement, what is the Initial Operating Capability forecast for Challenger 3.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to Question 12210 answered on 30 June which remains extant.

writtenQuestions.heading: Hawk Aircraft: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, with reference to the Defence Investment Plan, published on 30 June 2026, Table 7: Spending on the Air Domain, what is the scope of the £860m funding for the Hawk T1 and T2.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The £860 million is to maintain the safe operation of the Hawk T1 and Hawk T2 until the arrival of the Jet Training System. This will replace the Hawk T2 training aircraft that does not meet future training demands, ahead of the UK Military Flying Trainin...

writtenQuestions.heading: Sky Sabre
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, with reference to the Defence Investment Plan, published on 30 June 2026, page 28, what estimate he has made of the number of the Sky Sabre air defence missile systems operated by the Army.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan commits over £350 million to double the number of deployable Sky Sabre air defence missile systems operated by the Armed Forces. Key elements of this increase are already under contract and due to deliver by 2027. Sky Sabre rep...

writtenQuestions.heading: Ballistic Missile Defence: Unmanned Air Systems
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, with reference to the Defence Investment Plan, published on 30 June 2026, page 28 on Integrated Air and Missile Defence, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of CCAs on IAMD into the 2030s.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Strategic Defence Review committed to strengthening the UK's homeland Integrated Air and Missile Defence (IAMD), now realised through the Defence Investment Plan with £790 million of additional investment. This funding prioritises the sustainment of c...

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Weapons: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, pursuant to the answer of 29 June 2026, to question 11271 on Nuclear Weapons: Procurement, and with reference to the Defence Investment Plan, published in 30 June 2026, Table 3: Spending on the Defence Nuclear En
writtenQuestions.answerText: Funding for the Astraea programme is included within the £13 billion investment in the sovereign nuclear warhead programme between the 2026-27 and 2029-30 financial years, as announced in the 2026 Defence Investment Plan. That investment also includes sus...

writtenQuestions.heading: Trident Submarines
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what plans he has to define the requirement for the post-Dreadnought strategic deterrent.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As announced in the Defence Investment Plan, this Government will invest over £20 billion more in the Defence Nuclear Enterprise over the next four years than the previous four years to modernise and sustain our nuclear deterrent, the cornerstone of our n...

writtenQuestions.heading: Unmanned Air Systems
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what is the full scope of Project PANTHEON.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Project PANTHEON is a spiral development programme designed to transition the majority of Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (ISR), Strike and Logistic Lift effects currently delivered by Royal Navy Merlin and Wildcat helicopters to Vertical an...

writtenQuestions.heading: Arms Trade: Exports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what is the list if export deals closed by National Armaments International since its formation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence does not publish a list of all export deals secured, for security and operational reasons. However, we do publish annual defence export statistics. The most recent, covering 2024 can be found at: https://www.gov.uk/government/stati...

writtenQuestions.heading: Unmanned Marine Systems: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, with reference to the Defence Investment Plan, published on 30 June 2026, page 26, what is the full scope of the autonomous seabed warfare systems capability he intends to procure in order to protect critical nat
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan includes investment in seabed warfare capabilities as part of the Royal Navy’s wider Atlantic Bastion programme and Hybrid Navy transformation. This includes enhancements to RFA PROTEUS and the introduction of new capabilities ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Uncrewed Systems
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, with reference to the Defence Investment Plan, published on 30 June 2026, page 26, under which projects or programmes will the rapid development and production of uncrewed ground vehicles commence.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan outlined £150 million to commence a programme to rapidly develop and produce uncrewed vehicles for the Army, through UK industry. This will capitalise on, and accelerate, the hitherto industry-led research & development of ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Army
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, with reference to the Defence Investment Plan, published on 30 June 2026, page 26, what is the full scope of the land lethality pipeline programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan commits £400 million to deliver a suite of inexpensive, expendable autonomous systems and weapons to rapidly enhance the lethality of the Army, Commando Force and Special Forces. The Land Lethality Pipeline will be focused on d...

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, with reference to the Defence Investment Plan, published on 30 June 2026, page 19, by what process will the Ministry of Defence weight procurement decisions towards British companies.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the Defence Investment Plan we will bring forward a package of “backing British” measures to ensure that Defence spending is driving value back into local communities, while strengthening our long-term national security and driving sustainab...
